steps.title: Identify the structure of the inserted phrase
steps.content: The phrase according to legal researchers James Melton and Tom Ginsburg is placed between the subject Iceland's constitution and the verb contains. It breaks the main clause into two parts.

steps.title: Apply the rule for inserted phrases
steps.content:
When a phrase is inserted between a subject and verb, it needs commas on both sides to mark its boundaries.

One comma already appears after constitution to open the phrase, so a closing comma must come after the phrase and before contains.

Option C provides this closing comma, creating the correct structure: Iceland's constitution, according to legal researchers James Melton and Tom Ginsburg, contains … The other options either omit the closing comma or introduce a period, breaking the sentence.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
